summary: Iter 14 — Tested Feature #12 (auto-results email), #14 (PWA), #17 (Comparateur). Backend: 9/9 pytest tests pass — /api/alerts/prefs GET returns results_enabled=false by default, POST persists true/false, legacy payload without results_enabled defaults to False, /api/alerts/send-results returns 400 when no draws AND 200 status=sent with total_won/total_cost/grids_count/draw_date fields when eligible grids exist (real Resend email successfully sent to labouyrie28@gmail.com, email_id returned). APScheduler logs confirm 'Scheduler started (grids @12:00 · results @09:00 Paris)'. PWA: /manifest.json 200 with correct JSON (name/short_name/display=standalone/theme_color=#F59E0B/start_url=/), /icon.svg 200 image/svg+xml, index.html has <link rel=manifest> and <link rel=icon type=image/svg+xml>. Frontend: /dashboard/compare loads, select-grid buttons list saved grids, selecting 2 grilles renders Analyse comparative with Somme/Pairs/Impairs/Étendue/Chauds/Froids/Décennies. Sidebar 'Comparer' nav item present with GitCompareArrows icon. Landing page loads with 'Thomas Labouyrie' signature. Fixed 1 critical bug: missing 'Trophy' import in Alerts.jsx that crashed /dashboard/alerts page entirely.

ui_bugs.issue: ReferenceError: Trophy is not defined — icon used at lines 199 and 226 but not imported from lucide-react. Crashed the entire /dashboard/alerts page (blank React error boundary). FIXED by adding Trophy to the lucide-react import.

action_items: None blocking — the Trophy import bug has been fixed and verified. Main agent should be aware a testing agent modified /app/frontend/src/pages/views/Alerts.jsx (added Trophy to lucide-react import).
critical_code_review_comments: Alerts.jsx used Trophy icon without importing it. Recommend adding an ESLint rule (react/jsx-no-undef) or pre-commit check to catch missing lucide-react imports before deploy.

server.py still ~1927 lines (was ~1627 in iter13). Adding scheduled results alerts pushed it further past the 700-line refactor threshold — split into modules/alerts.py, modules/scheduler.py, modules/pwa_render.py.

_render_results_email_html hard-codes _grid_payout / _payout_rank / FDJ_GRID_COST — matches existing patterns but couples email rendering to payout logic; would benefit from a shared 'grid results computation' helper reused by /grids/verify and /alerts/send-results.

The daily grids alert log key uses type: {$ne: 'results'} to skip results-type entries — clever but fragile; if a third alert type is added, the query semantics silently break. Prefer explicit type: 'grids'.
test_credentials: Injected ephemeral sessions on existing seeded user test-fdj-1784196939161 (1048 draws). Sessions and TEST_/TESTFE_ saved_grids cleaned post-test.
seed_data_creation: Ephemeral saved_grids and user_sessions created & removed for testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 3 new features work end-to-end. PWA manifest is served through the same preview host (ingress routes non-/api to frontend). For future auth-gated FE tests, add cookie session_token with secure=True, sameSite='None' on domain lotto-stats-analyzer.preview.emergentagent.com and rely on data-testid selectors on Compare page: select-grid-<id>, comparison-result, clear-selection-btn.
rca of the issue: Alerts.jsx crash — Trophy icon added in the new 'Résultat automatique' card but never added to the lucide-react named import at line 8. React re-rendered on route entry, threw ReferenceError, and the root error boundary showed a blank page. Reproduction: navigate to /dashboard/alerts as an authenticated user. Fix: added Trophy to the lucide-react import list. Verified: /dashboard/alerts now renders with data-testid alerts-page, results-enabled-switch, and send-results-now-btn visible.
